commit.message:
Fix game hang by correcting CPU branch timing

Branch taken (+1) and page-crossing (+2) cycle penalties were lost because
branch() incremented this.cycles but execute() overwrote it with the base
cost. Use a branchPenalty field that gets added after execute() returns.
Also remove temporary debug instrumentation from PPU and NesEmulator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add component diagram in draw.io format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game hang after screensaver by delivering APU frame counter IRQs to CPU

The APU's isIrqPending() was never checked in the main loop, so games
relying on APU IRQs for timing (e.g. Double Dragon & Battletoads) would
spin indefinitely waiting for an interrupt that never fired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add APU implementation for sound support

Implement NES Audio Processing Unit with pulse, triangle, and noise
channels, wired into the CPU bus and main emulation loop. Audio output
uses javax.sound.sampled at 44100 Hz.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game freeze after title screen by adding NMI edge detection, PRG RAM, and DMA NMI handling

- Trigger NMI immediately when game enables NMI via PPUCTRL while VBlank is active
- Add 8KB PRG RAM at $6000-$7FFF so game state reads/writes work correctly
- Check for NMI during DMA transfer to avoid missing VBlank interrupts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add README with architecture overview, setup, and API docs

commit.message:
Implement notification system with server cluster, workers, emulators, and load testing

- Notification server: REST API, input validation, template rendering, Redis caching, RabbitMQ routing
- Notification worker: 4 dedicated queue consumers (APNS/FCM/SMS/EMAIL) with retry logic
- Third-party emulator: simulates delivery services with 95% success rate
- Device emulator: receives notifications, WebSocket broadcast, REST stats
- Load client: seeds test data, multi-threaded load test with p95/p99 reporting
- Infrastructure: PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, Prometheus, Grafana with pre-provisioned dashboard
- Docker Compose with service health checks and replicas
- 27 unit tests covering validation, services, controllers, consumers
- Component diagram in drawio format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Implement Extended Link Opaque LSA parser and database detail display

- Parse Extended Link TLV (RFC 7684 §3) with Adj-SID (RFC 8665 §5)
  and LAN Adj-SID (RFC 8665 §6) Sub-TLVs
- Add show database detail output for Extended Link LSAs
- Clean up remaining debug println statements in ifsm.rs and inst.rs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add create task dialog, auto-save, multi-file drop, and comments/data fixes

- Add CreateTaskDialog: when filters are active, Enter opens a dialog instead
  of inserting an inline block, supporting #hashtag and @mention parsing
- Add debounced auto-save (3s default) for inline note title edits
- Support multi-file drag & drop in BlockNoteEditor with parallel uploads,
  and inject delete buttons on file blocks
- Simplify comments UI: remove reply/resolve threading, flatten to a linear
  comment list with compact layout and toast on save
- Make comments fully optimistic: instant UI updates with pending-mutation
  guard to prevent realtime subscription from overwriting local state
- Fix foreign key violations: await note insert in Supabase before emitting
  label/assignee events in createNote and createNoteAfter
- Add optimistic assignee cache updates via event subscription
- Change default note sort from sort_order to created_at descending
- Fix GCal calendar fetch deduplication with one-shot ref
- Add is_default field to Contact type, fix Supabase type casts
- Remove note_comments updated_at DB trigger (managed in app code)
- Add i18n keys for create task dialog and comment saved toast (en/es/pt)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: correct api test mocks for jsdom environment and flexible header matching

Use jsdom's native window instead of stubbing globalThis, and match headers
with objectContaining to avoid brittleness from additional default headers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement OSPF SR TLV parsing and database detail display

- Move SidLabelTlv to packet-utils crate for sharing between isis-packet and ospf-packet
- Add OSPF-style SID/Label Sub-TLV parser (2+2 byte header vs IS-IS 1+1)
- Parse RouterInfo TLVs: SID/Label Range, Local Block, Router Capability, Algorithm
- Implement Extended Prefix Opaque LSA parser (RFC 7684) with Prefix SID Sub-TLV (RFC 8665)
- Add "show ip ospf database detail" output for Opaque Area LSAs with SR details
- Clean up debug println statements in nfsm.rs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(model-3d): fill triangular gaps in Case 3 split-face extrude

When extruding a split face (Case 3: mixed moved/duplicated vertices),
non_coplanar edges were skipped for wall creation. But when one vertex
of such an edge is moved and the other duplicated, a triangular gap
appears. Now all edges with shifted endpoints get walls, with
deduplication to collapse quads with repeated vertices into triangles.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix admin log buttons + add security log filters

1. Fix 'Vider les logs' / 'Vider le journal' buttons (errors.html + security_log.html)
   - Root cause: inline onclick="..." attributes are blocked by CSP (script-src
     uses nonces; 'unsafe-inline' is not present and is ignored when a nonce is set)
   - Fix: replace onclick attributes with addEventListener() inside the
     nonce-tagged <script> block — consistent with CSP best practice
   - Add location.reload() after successful clear instead of manual DOM surgery
   - Traceback expand/collapse also moved to addEventListener (same fix)

2. Add client-side filters to /admin/security-log
   - Dropdown 'Utilisateur' : populated dynamically from data-user attributes;
     default = Tous
   - Dropdown 'Événement'   : populated dynamically from data-event attributes
     (LOGIN_SUCCESS, LOGIN_FAIL, 2FA_SUCCESS, etc.); default = Tous
   - Filtering is pure JS — no page reload, no new endpoint needed
   - Visible count badge updates live (e.g. "12 / 45 événements")
   - All JS inside a single nonce-tagged <script> block, no inline handlers

3. Security compliance unchanged:
   - CSRF token auto-attached by base.html fetch interceptor (no change needed)
   - All new JS is inside nonce="{{csp_nonce}}" script blocks
   - No new server endpoints (purely client-side filtering)
   - @superadmin_required + rate limiting remain on existing clear endpoints

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Merge 11 individual AI tools into 2 consolidated components (Writer and
Analyzer), reducing Write tab tool count from 18 to 12. Add browser-side
file-to-text extraction (PDF, DOCX, XLSX, CSV) to all AI tools. Fix
language drift in small quantized models by adding default English
instruction to all 31 AI prompts.

commit.message:
fix(tensor): address integer overflow, zero-batch, and expect panics (#361)

- Fix integer overflow in narrow() bounds check using checked_add (#290)
- Fix tril()/triu() invalid iteration on zero-sized batch dimensions
  by returning empty tensor early when total elements is 0 (#291, #292)
- Improve .expect() messages for GPU safety (#293)
- Fix select() offset overflow using checked_mul/checked_add (#294)
- Fix extent calculation overflow in from_vec/view_as_strided using
  isize::try_from and checked_mul (#295)

Closes #290, Closes #291, Closes #292, Closes #293, Closes #294, Closes #295

commit.message:
fix: preserve document properties, resource names, and task item structure on round-trip

- Fix taskItem content: use inline nodes directly instead of wrapping in
  paragraph, matching LegendKeeper's expected schema
- Fix task list parse order: check `- [ ]` before `- ` to avoid matching
  as bullet list
- Preserve all document properties (isFullWidth, calendarId, etc.)
  through frontmatter using generic spread instead of explicit fields
- Add index signature to LkDocument for extra LK properties
- Store original resource name in frontmatter (lk.name) so names with
  colons and other filesystem-unsafe characters survive the round-trip
- Add tests for task item inline content, document property preservation,
  and resource name round-tripping

- Updated MAME device status for MN19413

commit.message:
fix: data quality issues in benchmarking pipeline (#25)

* fix: populate token counts in terminus-tb agent result

The return at lines 129-134 omitted input_tokens/output_tokens even
though they were already extracted from agent_result into metadata.
Extract them before the return and pass to AgentRunResult.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add power-based energy fallback for sparse telemetry samples

GPU energy was missing for 13-96% of queries because cumulative energy
counters require ≥2 samples for a delta. Add _estimate_energy_from_power
helper (energy ≈ avg_power × duration) as fallback at query, turn, and
correlation levels. Cumulative delta is still preferred when available.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: re-raise openhands-tb agent failures for correct completion tracking

The except block in _run_terminalbench swallowed perform_task exceptions,
causing AgentRunResult to be returned with 0 tokens and empty content.
AgenticRunner then marked these as completed=True. Save the exception
and re-raise after lm_inference_end fires so failed runs propagate to
the runner and get completed=False.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: mock terminal_bench module in tests for CI compatibility

CI doesn't have terminal_bench installed. Use sys.modules patching
instead of direct module attribute patching to avoid ModuleNotFoundError.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: bump httpx to 0.27.2 to resolve anthropic socket_options error, swap model to sonnet-4-6

anthropic>=0.40.0 uses socket_options in AsyncHTTPTransport which requires
httpx>=0.25.0. The pinned httpx==0.24.1 lacked this parameter causing
AsyncHTTPTransport.__init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'socket_options'.

Updated httpx to 0.27.2 (compatible with gql 3.5.0, supabase 2.3.4, postgrest).

Also updated Claude model from claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 to claude-sonnet-4-6
across all references in the LinkedIn search step (function, pipeline, debug panel).

commit.message:
Fix interference detection: apply assembly Location transforms to parts

The interference checker was comparing all parts at the origin (0,0,0)
instead of at their actual assembly positions. `_iter_assembly_parts`
extracted `child.obj` (the raw, untransformed shape) but ignored
`child.loc` (the Location transform from `assy.add(..., loc=...)`).

This meant that modifying datum position variables (SHAFT_Z, MOTOR_X,
etc.) had zero effect on interference results — the volumes were
identical to 4 decimal places across every build.

Fix: apply `solid.moved(loc)` to each part before boolean intersection.
Add VERIFY_INTERFERENCE.py for quick local validation with CadQuery.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(skills): use named reference instead of this in loadMany

Prevents this-binding loss when loadMany is destructured from the
registry object.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: tensor size mismatch in Unsloth GRPOTrainer compute_loss

- Add max_prompt_length=512 to GRPOConfig so all prompts are padded
  to uniform length, preventing off-by-one completion extraction
- Set tokenizer.padding_side="left" (required for batched generation
  with decoder-only models)
- Set num_iterations=1 explicitly to avoid re-forward pass shape drift

Fixes: RuntimeError: size of tensor a (21) must match size of tensor b (20)

[ENG-3341] Reduce ERCOT 60 Day Disclosure Memory Usage (#806)

* Reduce sced_resource_as_offers peak memory with pg_array output format

Add extract_curve_as_pg_string() that produces PG array strings directly
from numeric columns, avoiding Python list-of-lists (~15x less memory per
row). Thread output_format parameter through the SCED disclosure call chain
(get_60_day_sced_disclosure -> _handle_60_day_sced_disclosure ->
process_sced_resource_as_offers).

When output_format="pg_array", source price columns are dropped
progressively after each AS type extraction to reduce peak memory further.
Defaults to "list" for backwards compatibility.

Benchmark: sced_resource_as_offers peak drops from ~9.4 GB to ~2.1 GB.
